title: cydonia cydonia subvar. lusitanica
intro: Cydonia cydonia subvar. lusitanica, also known as quince, is a deciduous shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible fruit. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation, thanks to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cydonia cydonia subvar. lusitanica include its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), improve heart health, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of quince, such as tea and infusions, are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties, and it can also be used in jams, jellies, and other culinary applications.
constituents: Cydonia cydonia subvar. lusitanica has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which exhibit a range of medicinal properties. These constituents, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making them effective in treating various health conditions, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The plant's bioactive compounds, including luteolin, apigenin, and rutin, also demonstrate analgesic and anti-cancer properties, providing relief from pain and potentially inhibiting the growth of cancer cells. By harnessing the medicinal potential of Cydonia cydonia subvar. lusitanica, individuals can benefit from improved overall health, enhanced well-being, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
